title: Soy candle — neroli and ylang
subtitle: A rich, exotic soy wax container candle scented with neroli and ylang ylang fragrance oil
excerpt: Neroli and ylang ylang fragrance oil sits in the rich, tropical floral family: sweet, deep, and slightly green. Ylang ylang has a strong scent throw, so this blend is formulated at 6% fragrance load rather than the standard 7% to avoid the fragrance being overbearing in a small space. Suited to bedrooms and bathrooms.
sourceNotes: Soy wax container candle method: Bramble Berry candle-making guide (2023). Neroli (Citrus aurantium) and ylang ylang (Cananga odorata) fragrance oil: rich tropical floral family. Ylang ylang has a very strong scent throw; reduced load prevents the scent becoming cloying. Fragrance load 6% of wax weight. Pour temperature 55 to 60°C.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 candle (200 ml glass jar, ~150 g finished wax)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale wax and fragrance proportionally for multiple jars. Keep fragrance load at 6% for this blend.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure for at least 3 days before the first burn.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Two years away from direct sunlight. Fragrance fades after around two years; wax is stable indefinitely.

glossaryTerms.definition: The percentage of fragrance oil by wax weight. Reduced to 6% here because ylang ylang has a very strong natural scent throw.
The first burn. Allow the melt pool to reach the jar edge before extinguishing to set the burn pattern.
When a candle burns straight down the centre, leaving a ring of unburned wax at the edges. Caused by extinguishing before the melt pool reaches the jar edge on the first burn.

content.text: Neroli and ylang ylang fragrance oil is in the tropical floral family: rich, sweet, and slightly heady. Ylang ylang on its own can be overpowering in a room; the neroli note brightens and lifts the blend. The
here is reduced to 6% rather than the standard 7% to keep the scent throw noticeable without being heavy in a small room. This candle suits bedrooms and bathrooms.
Melt wax over a simmering-water bath on low heat only. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen-fire blanket within reach.

items.prepNote: 6% of wax weight; add at 55 to 60°C
Pre-tabbed cotton wick for a 65 mm jar

content.text: Wick, melt, add fragrance, and pour

Centre the pre-tabbed wick in the jar. Melt soy wax in the candle pitcher over a simmering-water bath to 65°C. Remove from heat. When cooled to 55 to 60°C, add the fragrance oil and stir for 2 minutes. Pour slowly into the jar, leaving 10 mm headspace. Tap once to release air bubbles.
Leave undisturbed for 3 to 5 days. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
. Allow the melt pool to reach the jar edge before extinguishing. Early extinguishing causes
Two years away from direct sunlight. Store with a lid or cover.
